response.justification: A strong, versatile senior/staff engineer with 10+ years across full-stack, backend, and embedded work, plus recent hands-on exposure to MCP and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq. However, the role requires 8+ years building production AI/GenAI solutions, and the candidate's demonstrable GenAI experience appears limited to roughly the last year. There is no evidence of Python depth, vector databases, RAG architectures, or named multi-agent frameworks, all of which are core to this position. The cloud (AWS) and TypeScript background and the customer-facing consulting history are genuine pluses, making this a borderline case worth a screening conversation to probe actual GenAI depth.
strengths: 10+ years senior/staff-level engineering with strong TypeScript/Node.js and AWS experience
Recent hands-on work with MCP and agentic-workflow frameworks aligned to the role
Customer-facing consulting and technical-leadership/mentoring track record (talks, workshops, courses)
concerns: Production GenAI/AI experience appears to be ~1 year, well short of the required 8+ years building AI solutions
No demonstrated experience with vector databases or RAG architectures, and no named multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI)
Little evidence of Python; AI/data-pipeline and LLM-evaluation experience is not clearly shown

key_factors.factor: Missing RAG / vector DB / multi-agent expertise
Strong senior/staff engineering with TS, AWS, and MCP/agentic exposure
